stories.headline: TRM Labs: North Korea-linked hackers drove 76% of 2026 crypto thefts, $577M in 4 months
stories.tldr: TRM Labs says DPRK-linked groups account for roughly 76% of all crypto theft losses in 2026, stealing about $577M in four months.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A single state actor now dominates on-chain theft, raising the bar for exchange and DeFi opsec and pulling US sanctions enforcement deeper into crypto.
the_details: DPRK groups stole an estimated $577M across exchanges, DeFi, and cross-chain bridges from January through April 2026.
TRM ties the surge to in-person social-engineering campaigns, including the $285M Drift drain on April 1.
North Korea has now stolen more than $6B since 2017, per TRM's running tally.

stories.headline: Block discloses 28,355 BTC stack worth $2.2B, $692M held on its own balance sheet
stories.tldr: Jack Dorsey's Block reported 28,355 BTC under management as of end-Q1, with 8,997 BTC ($692M) sitting on the corporate balance sheet.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Block joins MicroStrategy and Tesla in normalizing nine-figure BTC treasuries at public fintechs, anchoring institutional demand even as ETFs cool.
the_details: Block held 19,357 BTC (~$1.5B) on behalf of customers and 8,997 BTC (~$692M) as a corporate asset.
Disclosure landed in Block's Q1 2026 results filed April 30.
Total stack of 28,355 BTC was valued at roughly $2.2B at quarter-end.

stories.headline: Spot XRP ETFs lock up 769M tokens; Goldman Sachs surfaces as top holder
stories.tldr: Five US spot XRP ETFs have absorbed 769M XRP, with Goldman Sachs disclosed as one of the largest institutional holders.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: An XRP wrapper on Wall Street's biggest balance sheets cements the SEC-CFTC view that major tokens are commodities, not securities.
the_details: Cumulative spot XRP ETF holdings reached 769M tokens as of April 29 disclosures.
Goldman Sachs filings show it as a top-tier holder across the issuer cohort.
Inflows coincide with a Rakuten Pay XRP partnership that pushed sentiment into Santiment's FOMO zone.

stories.headline: State Street to launch tokenized fund servicing from Luxembourg by year-end
stories.tldr: State Street said it will stand up tokenized fund servicing out of Luxembourg before the end of 2026, via State Street Investment Services.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A multi-trillion-dollar custodian putting fund infrastructure on-chain is a milestone for tokenized real-world assets and EU MiCA-aligned plumbing.
the_details: Service will run from State Street Investment Services in Luxembourg, targeting EU-domiciled funds.
Launch planned by year-end 2026, per April 30 announcement.
Expands State Street's existing digital-asset custody pilots into core fund admin.

stories.headline: Wasabi Protocol drained for $4.55M after deployer admin key compromise
stories.tldr: Perp DEX Wasabi Protocol lost about $4.55M after attackers seized its deployer key and swapped vault contracts on Ethereum and Base.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Yet another single-key compromise — not a smart-contract bug — underscoring how operational security is now the dominant DeFi attack surface.
the_details: Attacker upgraded vault contracts to malicious versions across wWETH, sUSDC, wBITCON, wPEPE, and Long Pool on Ethereum.
Base-side vaults sUSDC, wWETH, sBTC, sVIRTUAL, sAERO, and sBRETT were also drained.
Loss pushes 2026 cumulative DeFi exploit total above $770M across 30+ incidents.
